items.headline: Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby enters treatment program for a gambling addiction, school says
items.description: LUBBOCK, Texas (AP) — Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby enters treatment program for a gambling addiction, school says.

items.headline: Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby enters treatment program for a gambling addiction following transfer
items.description:
LUBBOCK, Texas (AP) — Texas Tech quarterback Brendan Sorsby has taken an indefinite leave of absence from the team to enter a residential treatment program for a gambling addiction.
Texas Tech and Sorsby announced the move in a statement released on Monday.

items.headline: Sabalenka avoids Madrid Open virus scare and Osaka upset. Gauff loses
items.description:
MADRID (AP) — Tennis players are facing an unknown opponent at the Madrid Open.
A stomach virus or food poisoning has affected Coco Gauff, Marin Cilic and a few others, causing some concern.

items.headline: Modric suffers fractured cheekbone, will go under the knife: AC Milan

items.headline: Joel Embiid made a gutsy return from an appendectomy. The 76ers are still in trouble against Boston
items.description:
PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Joel Embiid presumed he had a wretched stomach bug that hit him hard for a day or two on the Philadelphia 76ers' road trip earlier this month in Texas.
The 7-footer from Cameroon became so debilitated by the ailment that he struggled walking, stayed awake deep into the night and even going to the bathroom became a chore. Embiid was finally forced to tell team officials this sickness was worse than food poisoning or any other malady he suspected, and he required a hospital visit.

items.headline: This Date in Baseball - Hank Aaron connected off Gaylord Perry for his 600th career home run
items.description:
April 28
1901 — Cleveland pitcher Bock Baker gave up a record 23 singles as the Chicago White Sox beat the Indians 13-1.
